revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[大島]]にある[[神社]]である。大島諏訪明神とも呼ばれる。 =...'
revision.text:
'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[大島]]にある[[神社]]である。大島諏訪明神とも呼ばれる。 == 祭神 == *御穂須々美命（みほすすみのみこと） *[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] [[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。
== 歴史 == 覚心師によって、永正年間（1504～1521年）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、宝永7年（1710年）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。延喜19年(919)、延喜式神明帳に記載された相模国13座のうちの1社、「石楯尾神社」のことではないかといわれているが定かではない。
== 祭事 == * 例祭・[[獅子舞]] ** 八月の最終日曜日 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。 剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。
== 交通 == * JR橋本駅南口、JR相模原駅南口から上大島行きバス、上大島下車。
== 周辺 == * [[長徳寺(相模原市)]]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 歴史 == 覚心師によって、永正年間（1504～1521年）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、宝永7年（1710年）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。延喜19年(919)、延喜式神明帳に記載された相模国13座のうちの1社、「石楯尾神社」のことではないかといわれているが定かではない。
== 祭事 == * 例祭・[[獅子舞]] ** 八月の最終日曜日 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。 剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。
== 交通 == * JR橋本駅南口、JR相模原駅南口から上大島行きバス、上大島下車。
== 周辺 == * [[長徳寺(相模原市)]]

{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪神社<small>（諏訪明神）</small> |画像= [[File:Oshima-swamyolin1.JPG|250px|]] |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 御穂須々美命 |社格 = |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神 |例祭 = 八月の最終日曜日 }}
'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島にある[[神社]]である。大島諏訪明神とも呼ばれる。
== 祭神・境内社 == *御穂須々美命（みほすすみのみこと） *境内・八坂社：[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。
== 歴史 == [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の社号標]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（1504〜1521年）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（1710年）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年(919年)、延喜式神明帳に記載された相模国13座のうちの1社、「[[石楯尾神社]]」のことではないかといわれているが定かではない。
== 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] ** 八月の最終日曜日（本来は8月26日） 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。
== 交通 == * JR橋本駅南口、JR相模原駅南口から上大島行きバス、上大島下車。
== 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=617&cd=1208222&scd=&npg=0 諏訪明神]（神奈川県神社庁サイト）

== 境内社 == 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。
== 歴史 == [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の社号標]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（1504〜1521年）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（1710年）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年(919年)、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載された[[相模国]]13座のうちの1社、「[[石楯尾神社]]」は当社のことではないかといわれているが定かではない。
== 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] ** 8月の最終日曜日（本来は8月26日） 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。
== 交通 == * JR橋本駅南口、JR相模原駅南口から上大島行きバス、上大島下車。
== 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]]

{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわみようしん}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:緑区 (相模原市)]]

{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪明神 |画像= [[File:Oshima-swamyolin1.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 御穂須々美命 |社格 = 式内社（小） |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神、お諏訪さま |例祭 = 8月の最終日曜日 }} '''諏訪明神'''（すわみょうじん）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島にある[[神社]]である。「大島諏訪明神」、あるいは「お諏訪さま」とも呼ばれる。[[相模原市教育委員会]]発行『相模原風土記稿』（神社編）では「諏訪神社」と表記されている[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm 諏訪明神]（ルーツ座間：野火のコラム）。 == 祭神 == * 御穂須々美命（みほすすみのみこと） * 境内・八坂社：[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] == 境内社 == 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。 == 歴史 == [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の社号標
「石楯尾神社」とある]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（1504〜1521年）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（1710年）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年(919年)、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載された[[相模国]]13座のうちの1社、「[[石楯尾神社]]」は当社のことではないか（[[論社]]）といわれているが定かではない。 == 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] ** 8月の最終日曜日（本来は8月26日） 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。 == 交通 == * JR[[橋本駅 (神奈川県)|橋本駅]]南口またはJR[[相模原駅]]南口から「上大島」行きバス、「上大島」下車。 == 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]] == 注釈 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=617&cd=1208222&scd=&npg=0 諏訪明神]（神奈川県神社庁サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわみようしん}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:緑区 (相模原市)]]

{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪明神 |画像= [[File:Oshima-swamyolin1.JPG|280px]] |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 御穂須々美命 |社格 = 式内社（小） |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神、お諏訪さま |例祭 = 8月の最終日曜日 }} [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の社額
「'''石楯尾神社'''」とある]] '''諏訪明神'''（すわみょうじん）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島にある[[神社]]である。「大島諏訪明神」、あるいは「お諏訪さま」とも呼ばれる。なお、[[相模原市教育委員会]]発行『相模原風土記稿』（神社編）では「'''諏訪神社'''」と表記されている[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm 諏訪明神]（ルーツ座間：野火のコラム）。 == 祭神 == * 御穂須々美命（みほすすみのみこと） * 境内・八坂社：[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] == 境内社 == 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（1504〜1521年）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（1710年）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年(919年)、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載された[[相模国]]13座のうちの1社、「[[石楯尾神社]]」は当社のことではないか（[[論社]]）といわれているが定かではない。 == 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] - 8月の最終日曜日（本来は[[8月26日]]） *: 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。 == 交通 == * JR[[橋本駅 (神奈川県)|橋本駅]]南口またはJR[[相模原駅]]南口から「上大島」行きバス、「上大島」下車。 == 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[相模国の式内社一覧]] * [[石楯尾神社]] * [[諏訪神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=617&cd=1208222&scd=&npg=0 諏訪明神]（神奈川県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわみようしん}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪明神 |画像= [[File:Oshima-swamyolin1.JPG|280px]] |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 御穂須々美命 |社格 = 式内社（小） |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神、お諏訪さま |例祭 = 8月の最終日曜日 }} [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の[[扁額]]
「'''石楯尾神社'''」とある]] '''諏訪明神'''（すわみょうじん）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島にある[[神社]]である。「大島諏訪明神」、あるいは「お諏訪さま」とも呼ばれる。なお、[[相模原市教育委員会]]発行『相模原風土記稿』（神社編）では「'''諏訪神社'''」と表記されている[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm 諏訪明神]（ルーツ座間：野火のコラム）。 == 祭神 == * 御穂須々美命（みほすすみのみこと） * 境内・八坂社：[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] == 境内社 == 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（1504〜1521年）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（1710年）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年(919年)、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載された[[相模国]]13座のうちの1社、「[[石楯尾神社]]」は当社のことではないか（[[論社]]）といわれているが定かではない。 == 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] - 8月の最終日曜日（本来は[[8月26日]]） *: 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。 == 交通 == * JR[[橋本駅 (神奈川県)|橋本駅]]南口またはJR[[相模原駅]]南口から「上大島」行きバス、「上大島」下車。 == 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[相模国の式内社一覧]] * [[石楯尾神社]] * [[諏訪神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=617&cd=1208222&scd=&npg=0 諏訪明神]（神奈川県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわみようしん}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪明神 |画像= [[File:Oshima-swamyolin1.JPG|280px]] |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 御穂須々美命 |社格 = 式内社（小） |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神、お諏訪さま |例祭 = 8月の最終日曜日 }} [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の[[扁額]]
「'''石楯尾神社'''」とある]] '''諏訪明神'''（すわみょうじん）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島にある[[神社]]である。「大島諏訪明神」、あるいは「お諏訪さま」とも呼ばれる。なお、[[相模原市教育委員会]]発行『相模原風土記稿』（神社編）では「'''諏訪神社'''」と表記されている[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm 諏訪明神]（ルーツ座間：野火のコラム）。 == 祭神 == * [[御穂須須美命|御穂須々美命]]（みほすすみのみこと） * 境内・八坂社：[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] == 境内社 == 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（1504〜1521年）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（1710年）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年（919年）、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載された[[相模国]]13座のうちの1社、「'''[[石楯尾神社]]'''」は当社のことではないか（[[論社]]）といわれているが定かではない。 == 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] - 8月の最終日曜日（本来は[[8月26日]]） *: 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。 == 交通 == * JR[[橋本駅 (神奈川県)|橋本駅]]南口またはJR[[相模原駅]]南口から「上大島」行きバス、「上大島」下車。 == 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == {{commonscat|Suwa-myojin (Oshima, Sagamihara)}} * [[相模国の式内社一覧]] * [[石楯尾神社]] * [[諏訪神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=617&cd=1208222&scd=&npg=0 諏訪明神]（神奈川県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわみようしん}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪明神 |画像= [[File:Oshima-swamyolin1.JPG|280px]] |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 御穂須々美命 |社格 = 式内社（小） |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神、お諏訪さま |例祭 = 8月の最終日曜日 }} [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の[[扁額]]
「'''石楯尾神社'''」とある]] '''諏訪明神'''（すわみょうじん）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島にある[[神社]]である。「大島諏訪明神」、あるいは「お諏訪さま」とも呼ばれる。なお、[[相模原市教育委員会]]発行『相模原風土記稿』（神社編）では「'''諏訪神社'''」と表記されている[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm 諏訪明神]（ルーツ座間：野火のコラム）{{リンク切れ|date=2021-11}}。 == 祭神 == * [[御穂須須美命|御穂須々美命]]（みほすすみのみこと） * 境内・八坂社：[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] == 境内社 == 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（[[1710年]]）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年（[[919年]]）、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載された[[相模国]]13座のうちの1社、「'''[[石楯尾神社]]'''」は当社のことではないか（[[論社]]）といわれている{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}が定かではない。 == 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] - 8月の最終日曜日（本来は[[8月26日]]） *: 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。 == 交通 == * JR[[橋本駅 (神奈川県)|橋本駅]]南口またはJR[[相模原駅]]南口から「上大島」行きバス、「上大島」下車。 == 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之67村里部高座郡巻之9|chapter=澁谷庄 大島村 諏訪社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == 関連項目 == {{commonscat|Suwa-myojin (Oshima, Sagamihara)}} * [[相模国の式内社一覧]] * [[石楯尾神社]] * [[諏訪神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=617&cd=1208222&scd=&npg=0 諏訪明神]（神奈川県神社庁） {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわみようしん}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪明神 |画像= [[File:Oshima-swamyolin1.JPG|280px]] |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{Coord|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-14|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 御穂須々美命 |社格 = 式内社（小） |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神、お諏訪さま |例祭 = 8月の最終日曜日 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture|label=諏訪明神|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の[[扁額]]

{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪明神 |画像= [[File:Oshima-swamyolin1.JPG|250px]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{Coord|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-14|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 御穂須々美命 |社格 = 式内社（小） |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神、お諏訪さま |例祭 = 8月の最終日曜日 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture|label=諏訪明神|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の[[扁額]]
「'''石楯尾神社'''」とある]] '''諏訪明神'''（すわみょうじん）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島にある[[神社]]である。「大島諏訪明神」、あるいは「お諏訪さま」とも呼ばれる。なお、[[相模原市教育委員会]]発行『相模原風土記稿』（神社編）では「'''諏訪神社'''」と表記されている[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm 諏訪明神]（ルーツ座間：野火のコラム）{{リンク切れ|date=2021-11}}。 == 祭神 == * [[御穂須須美命|御穂須々美命]]（みほすすみのみこと） * 境内・八坂社：[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] == 境内社 == 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（[[1710年]]）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年（[[919年]]）、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載された[[相模国]]13座のうちの1社、「'''[[石楯尾神社]]'''」は当社のことではないか（[[論社]]）といわれている{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}が定かではない。 == 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] - 8月の最終日曜日（本来は[[8月26日]]） *: 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。 == 交通 == * JR[[橋本駅 (神奈川県)|橋本駅]]南口またはJR[[相模原駅]]南口から「上大島」行きバス、「上大島」下車。 == 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之67村里部高座郡巻之9|chapter=澁谷庄 大島村 諏訪社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == 関連項目 == {{commonscat|Suwa-myojin (Oshima, Sagamihara)}} * [[相模国の式内社一覧]] * [[石楯尾神社]] * [[諏訪神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=617&cd=1208222&scd=&npg=0 諏訪明神]（神奈川県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわみようしん}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attriubtion, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]][[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪明神 |画像= [[File:Oshima-swamyolin1.JPG|250px]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{Coord|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-14|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 御穂須々美命 |社格 = 式内社（小） |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神、お諏訪さま |例祭 = 8月の最終日曜日 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture|label=諏訪明神|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の[[扁額]]
「'''石楯尾神社'''」とある]] '''諏訪明神'''（すわみょうじん）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島にある[[神社]]である。「大島諏訪明神」、あるいは「お諏訪さま」とも呼ばれる。なお、[[相模原市教育委員会]]発行『相模原風土記稿』（神社編）では「'''諏訪神社'''」と表記されている[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm 諏訪明神]（ルーツ座間：野火のコラム）{{リンク切れ|date=2021-11}}。 == 祭神 == * [[御穂須須美命|御穂須々美命]]（みほすすみのみこと） * 境内・八坂社：[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] == 境内社 == 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（[[1710年]]）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年（[[919年]]）、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載された[[相模国]]13座のうちの1社、「'''[[石楯尾神社]]'''」は当社のことではないか（[[論社]]）といわれている{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}が定かではない。 == 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] - 8月の最終日曜日（本来は[[8月26日]]） *: 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。 == 交通 == * JR[[橋本駅 (神奈川県)|橋本駅]]南口またはJR[[相模原駅]]南口から「上大島」行きバス、「上大島」下車。 == 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之67村里部高座郡巻之9|chapter=澁谷庄 大島村 諏訪社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == 関連項目 == {{commonscat|Suwa-myojin (Oshima, Sagamihara)}} * [[相模国の式内社一覧]] * [[石楯尾神社]] * [[諏訪神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=617&cd=1208222&scd=&npg=0 諏訪明神]（神奈川県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわみようしん}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

社殿 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{Coord|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-14|display=inline,title}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = 御穂須々美命 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 式内社（小） |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神、お諏訪さま | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = 8月の最終日曜日 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture|label=諏訪明神|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の[[扁額]]
「'''石楯尾神社'''」とある]] '''諏訪明神'''（すわみょうじん）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島にある[[神社]]である。「大島諏訪明神」、あるいは「お諏訪さま」とも呼ばれる。なお、[[相模原市教育委員会]]発行『相模原風土記稿』（神社編）では「'''諏訪神社'''」と表記されている[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm 諏訪明神]（ルーツ座間：野火のコラム）{{リンク切れ|date=2021-11}}。 == 祭神 == * [[御穂須須美命|御穂須々美命]]（みほすすみのみこと） * 境内・八坂社：[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] == 境内社 == 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（[[1710年]]）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年（[[919年]]）、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載された[[相模国]]13座のうちの1社、「'''[[石楯尾神社]]'''」は当社のことではないか（[[論社]]）といわれている{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}が定かではない。 == 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] - 8月の最終日曜日（本来は[[8月26日]]） *: 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。 == 交通 == * JR[[橋本駅 (神奈川県)|橋本駅]]南口またはJR[[相模原駅]]南口から「上大島」行きバス、「上大島」下車。 == 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之67村里部高座郡巻之9|chapter=澁谷庄 大島村 諏訪社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == 関連項目 == {{commonscat|Suwa-myojin (Oshima, Sagamihara)}} * [[相模国の式内社一覧]] * [[石楯尾神社]] * [[諏訪神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=617&cd=1208222&scd=&npg=0 諏訪明神]（神奈川県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed:すわみようしん}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪明神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image= [[File:Oshima-swamyolin1.JPG|250px]]
社殿 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島594 |位置 = {{Coord|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-14|display=inline,title}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = 御穂須々美命 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 式内社（小） |創建 = 永正年間（1504〜1521年） |本殿 = |別名 = 大島諏訪明神、お諏訪さま | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = 8月の最終日曜日 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture|label=諏訪明神|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|34|58|N|139|18|23.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居の手前から撮影]] [[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|鳥居の[[扁額]]
「'''石楯尾神社'''」とある]] '''諏訪明神'''（すわみょうじん）は、[[神奈川県]][[相模原市]][[緑区 (相模原市)|緑区]]大島にある[[神社]]である。「大島諏訪明神」、あるいは「お諏訪さま」とも呼ばれる。なお、[[相模原市教育委員会]]発行『相模原風土記稿』（神社編）では「'''諏訪神社'''」と表記されている[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm 諏訪明神]（ルーツ座間：野火のコラム）{{リンク切れ|date=2021-11}}。 == 祭神 == * [[御穂須須美命|御穂須々美命]]（みほすすみのみこと） * 境内・八坂社：[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売之命（やさかとめのみこと）]] == 境内社 == 境内社として八坂社・[[天満宮]]・[[水天宮]]があり、本殿の裏には蚕影社が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 覚心師によって、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）に創建されたと伝えられている。その後、[[宝永]]7年（[[1710年]]）に再建され、旧大島村、旧九沢村の総鎮守となる。[[延喜]]19年（[[919年]]）、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載された[[相模国]]13座のうちの1社、「'''[[石楯尾神社]]'''」は当社のことではないか（[[論社]]）といわれている{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}が定かではない。 == 祭事 == * [[例祭]]・[[獅子舞]] - 8月の最終日曜日（本来は[[8月26日]]） *: 獅子舞が奉納され、神奈川県指定[[重要無形民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]、相模原市登録無形民俗文化財となっている。剣獅子・巻獅子・玉獅子（雌獅子）の3頭の獅子と、赤鬼・天狗・道化役の岡崎が笛と唄手に合わせて舞う。 == 交通 == * JR[[橋本駅 (神奈川県)|橋本駅]]南口またはJR[[相模原駅]]南口から「上大島」行きバス、「上大島」下車。 == 周辺 == * [[長徳寺 (相模原市)]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之67村里部高座郡巻之9|chapter=澁谷庄 大島村 諏訪社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == 関連項目 == {{commonscat|Suwa-myojin (Oshima, Sagamihara)}} * [[相模国の式内社一覧]] * [[石楯尾神社]] * [[諏訪神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=617&cd=1208222&scd=&npg=0 諏訪明神]（神奈川県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed:すわみようしん}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:相模原市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:諏訪明神 (相模原市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]]

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|qq=|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|qq=|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|qq=|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|qq=|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|qq=|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|qq=|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja|長徳寺 (相模原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja|長徳寺 (相模原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}

* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column)[[:en:Link rot|Link broken]], November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ([[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on [[:en:August 26|August 26]]). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of [[:en:Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)]] or the south exit of [[:en:Sagamihara Station|Sagamihara Station]], take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column){{ill|Link rot|en|Link rot|WD=Q1193907|lt=Link broken}}, November 2021</ref>
