== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26}}). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of {{ill|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|WD=Q800895|lt=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)}} or the south exit of {{ill|Sagamihara Station|en|Sagamihara Station|WD=Q844696|lt=Sagamihara Station}}, take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column){{ill|Link rot|en|Link rot|WD=Q1193907|lt=Link broken}}, November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}}

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26}}). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of {{ill|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|WD=Q800895|lt=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)}} or the south exit of {{ill|Sagamihara Station|en|Sagamihara Station|WD=Q844696|lt=Sagamihara Station}}, take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column){{ill|Link rot|en|Link rot|WD=Q1193907|lt=Link broken}}, November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}}

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26}}). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of {{ill|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|WD=Q800895|lt=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)}} or the south exit of {{ill|Sagamihara Station|en|Sagamihara Station|WD=Q844696|lt=Sagamihara Station}}, take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja|長徳寺 (相模原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column){{ill|Link rot|en|Link rot|WD=Q1193907|lt=Link broken}}, November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}}

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26}}). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of {{ill|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|WD=Q800895|lt=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)}} or the south exit of {{ill|Sagamihara Station|en|Sagamihara Station|WD=Q844696|lt=Sagamihara Station}}, take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja|長徳寺 (相模原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column){{ill|Link rot|en|Link rot|WD=Q1193907|lt=Link broken}}, November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}}

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26}}). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of {{ill|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|WD=Q800895|lt=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)}} or the south exit of {{ill|Sagamihara Station|en|Sagamihara Station|WD=Q844696|lt=Sagamihara Station}}, take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja|長徳寺 (相模原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26}}).

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column){{ill|Link rot|en|Link rot|WD=Q1193907|lt=Link broken}}, November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}}

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26}}). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of {{ill|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|WD=Q800895|lt=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)}} or the south exit of {{ill|Sagamihara Station|en|Sagamihara Station|WD=Q844696|lt=Sagamihara Station}}, take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja|長徳寺 (相模原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Iwatateono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26}}). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Ihatate-no-Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082|1=Suwa Shrine}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column){{ill|Link rot|en|Link rot|WD=Q1193907|lt=Link broken|1=Link rot}}, November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726|1=Mihosusumi no Mikoto}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Ihatate-no-Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai|1=Lion dance}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26|1=August 26}}). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of {{ill|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|WD=Q800895|lt=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|1=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)}} or the south exit of {{ill|Sagamihara Station|en|Sagamihara Station|WD=Q844696|lt=Sagamihara Station|1=Sagamihara Station}}, take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja|長徳寺 (相模原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Ihatate-no-Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082|1=Suwa Shrine}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column){{ill|Link rot|en|Link rot|WD=Q1193907|lt=Link broken|1=Link rot}}, November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726|1=Mihosusumi no Mikoto}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Ihatate-no-Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai|1=Lion dance}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26|1=August 26}}). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of {{ill|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|WD=Q800895|lt=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|1=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)}} or the south exit of {{ill|Sagamihara Station|en|Sagamihara Station|WD=Q844696|lt=Sagamihara Station|1=Sagamihara Station}}, take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja|長徳寺 (相模原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Ihatate-no-Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

[[File:Oshima-swamyolin2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|View from in front of the torii]]
[[File:Oshima-swamyolin3.JPG|thumb|150px|right|Banners ({{ill|Hengaku|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hengaku|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) reading “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Ihatate-no-Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”]]
'''Suwa Myōjin''' (諏訪明神, すわみょうじん) is a Shinto shrine located on Oshima in Midori Ward, Sagamihara City, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as “Oshima Suwa Myōjin” or “Osuwa-sama”. In the publication 『{{ill|Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja|相模原風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamihara Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 (Shrine section) by the Sagamihara City Board of Education, it is referred to as “{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}”.<ref>[http://www.ne.jp/asahi/zama/nobi/new_page_2.htm]{{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082|1=Suwa Shrine}} (Roots Zama: Nobi Column){{ill|Link rot|en|Link rot|WD=Q1193907|lt=Link broken|1=Link rot}}, November 2021</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) ==
{{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}}
* {{ill|Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja|御穂須々美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q136769726|1=Mihosusumi no Mikoto}} (Mihosusumi-no-Mikoto)
* Within the shrine precincts at the Yasaka-sha: {{ill|Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売之命|lt=Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Tomeredomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Sub-shrines ==
* An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}

== History ==
According to tradition, this shrine was established by a monk of the Buddhist order (Kakushinshi) during the {{ill|Eishō era|ja|永正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1504–1521). It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Hōei 7|ja|宝永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1710) and became the collective tutelary shrine for the former Oshima Village and the former Kūsawa Village. It is also said that one of the 13 shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Sagami Province (【13 Zoa of Sagami】) is “{{ill|Ishikateo Shrine|ja|石楯尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikateo Shrine|1=Ihatate-no-Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586614}}”, which some scholars argue may refer to this shrine; however, this identification remains uncertain.<ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
* The annual festival and lion dance ({{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai|1=Lion dance}}) are held on the last Sunday in August (originally on {{ill|August 26|en|August 26|WD=Q2820|lt=August 26|1=August 26}}). 
* At the festival, a lion dance is performed, which has been designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kanagawa Prefecture and as a registered intangible folk cultural property by Sagamihara City. Three types of lions (sword lion, rolled lion, and ball lion [female lion]) are seen dancing in coordination with a flutist and chanter, along with roles for red ogre, tengu, and clowns portrayed by Okazaki.
== Transportation Access ==
* From either the south exit of {{ill|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|en|Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|WD=Q800895|lt=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)|1=Hashimoto Station (Kanagawa)}} or the south exit of {{ill|Sagamihara Station|en|Sagamihara Station|WD=Q844696|lt=Sagamihara Station|1=Sagamihara Station}}, take the bus bound for “Kami Oshima” and get off at “Kami Oshima.”

== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja|長徳寺 (相模原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku-ji (Sagamihara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
